title: Hugh of Lincoln
content:

title: Helen Of Kirkconnell
King Cophetua and the Beggar-Maid
content:
I read that once in Affrica
    A princely wight did raine,
    Who had to name Cophetua,
    As poets they did faine.

title: Fair Rosamond
content:
When as King Henry rulde this land,
    The second of that name,
    Besides the queene, he dearly lovde
    A faire and comely dame.

title: The Nut-brown Maid
content:
Be it right, or wrong, these men among

title: The Mermaid
content:
To yon fause stream that, near the sea,
    Hides mony an elf and plum,
    And rives wi' fearful din the stanes,
    A witless knicht did come

title: Waly, Waly, Love be Bonny
content:
O waly, waly up the bank,
    And waly, waly down the brae,
    And waly, waly yon burn side,
    Where I and my love were wont to gae.
    I

title: Chevy Chace
content:
God prosper long our noble king,
    Our liffes and safetyes all;
    A woefull hunting once there did
    In Chevy-Chace befall.

title: The Twa Corbies
content:
As I was walking all alane,
    I heard twa corbies making a maen:
    The tane unto the t'ither did say,
    "Whaur shall we gang and dine the

title: Sir Patrick Spens
content:
The king sits in Dunfermline town,
    Drinking the blude-red wine;
    "O whare will I get a skeely skipper,
    To sail this new ship of mine

title: Annan Water
content:
"Annan Water's wading deep,
    And my love Annie's wondrous bonny;
    I will keep my tryst to-night,
    And win the heart o' lovely Annie."

title: The Bailiff's Daughter of Islington
content:

title: Barbara Allen's Cruelty
content:
All in the merry month of May,
    When green buds they were swelling,
    Young Jemmy Grove on his death-bed lay
    For love o' Barbara Allen

title: Fair Margaret And Sweet William
content:
As it fell out on a long summer's day,
    Two lovers they sat on a hill;
    They sat together that long summer's day,
    And could not talk

title: Phillida And Corydon
content:
In the merrie moneth of Maye,
    In a morne by break of daye,
    With a troope of damselles playing
    Forthe 'I yode' forsooth a maying;

title: The Banks o' Yarrow
content:
Late at e'en, drinking the wine,

title: Flodden Field
content:
King Jamie hath made a vow,

    Upon Saint James his day.

title: The Douglas Tragedy

title: The Lament of the Border Widow
content:
My love he built me a bonny bower,

    A brawer bower ye ne'er did see,

title: Robin Hood and Allen-a-Dale
content:
Come listen to me, you gallants so free,

title: The Fause Lover
content:
A fair maid sat in her bower door,
    Wringing her lily hands;
    And by it came a sprightly youth,
    Fast tripping o'er the strands.

title: Robin Hood And Guy Of Gisborne
content:

title: The Battle of Otterburn
content:
THE FIRST FYTTE


    It fell about the Lammas tide,
    When husbands winn their hay,
    The doughty Douglas bound him to ride
    Into Engl

title: Young Waters
content:
About Yule, when the wind blew cool;
    And the round tables began,
    A' there is come to our king's court
    Mony a well-favoured man.
